commit.message:
fix: replace signal.alarm with threading timeout for Streamlit compatibility

signal.signal() only works in the main thread but Streamlit runs scripts
in worker threads. Use threading.Thread with join(timeout) instead.

commit.message:
fix: bundle API with tsup to resolve workspace .ts imports on Vercel

Prisma 7 and workspace packages export raw TypeScript files which
Node.js cannot import at runtime. Add tsup to bundle the API entry
point with workspace packages inlined and Prisma runtime externalized.
Use hono/vercel adapter and configure vercel.json for the serverless
function.

across all projects to 10.0.3 to resolve transitive dependency

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix HTTP/SSE transport for remote IP connections

- Disable MCP library's DNS rebinding protection (TransportSecuritySettings)
  which was rejecting non-localhost Host headers with 421 errors
- Replace Starlette middleware auth with raw ASGI wrapper that validates
  Bearer tokens before the request reaches FastMCP's middleware stack
- Add systemd user service setup instructions and management commands
- Document SSH caveats (ClearAllForwardings, LogLevel=ERROR) that prevent
  stderr noise from breaking the MCP JSON-RPC handshake
- Update Claude Code config to use `claude mcp add` CLI (writes to
  ~/.claude.json instead of ~/.claude/settings.json)
- Expand troubleshooting tables with HTTP/SSE-specific issues

fix: remediate 12 CLI bugs from E2E testing (#167)

- EDL update: strip `id` field before SDK create fallback
- BGP routing: omit empty `outbound_routes_for_services` list
- Remote network/service connection: remove `folder` leak into SDK create
- Snippet delete: `snippet_id=` → `object_id=`
- Variable show/delete: remove unsupported `snippet=`/`device=` kwargs from fetch()
- Folder show/delete: add None-check after fetch() returns None
- Security rule update: fix `log_setting` type conversion
- Anti-spyware profile: action format `"block"` → `{"block": {}}`
- Vulnerability protection profile: same action fix + add default `cve: ["any"]`
- Auth setting: fix CLI-to-SDK field mapping (`authentication_profile`)
- Jobs wait: extract `JobStatusData` from response wrapper
- Objects: "Created" → "Updated" message for update operations

Closes #166

refactor(async): convert batch 5 learner hooks to async fs

Modified files:
- src/hooks/learner/auto-invoke.ts: 4 functions → async
  - loadInvocationConfig: existsSync/readFileSync → access/readFile
  - initAutoInvoke: now async (calls loadInvocationConfig)
  - loadInvocationHistory: existsSync/readFileSync → access/readFile
  - getAggregatedStats: existsSync/readdirSync/readFileSync → access/readdir/readFile
- src/hooks/learner/queue-archiver.ts: 2 methods → async
  - archive: readFileSync/mkdirSync/readFileSync → readFile/mkdir/readFile
  - loadArchiveIds: readFileSync → readFile
- src/hooks/learner/reflection-cleanup.ts: 1 function → async
  - cleanupReflectionLog: readFileSync/copyFileSync → readFile/copyFile
- src/hooks/learner/reflection-archiver.ts: 1 function → async
  - archiveReflections: mkdirSync/readFileSync/appendFileSync/readFileSync → mkdir/readFile/appendFile/readFile

Impact: Fixed 17 ESLint errors (87 → 70)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(monitor): scope monitor-gh-actions.sh to PR branch (#1092)

* fix(monitor): scope monitor-gh-actions.sh to PR branch (bead xswt)

$1 is documented and called everywhere as a PR number, but the script was
treating it as a run ID fallback and fetching active runs repo-wide, causing
unrelated workflows to be monitored.

- Rename $1 from REQUESTED_RUN_ID → PR_NUMBER (matches all call sites)
- Move PR_NUMBER to $1 (was $2); remove the unused run-ID-fallback path
- When a PR number is given, resolve the branch with `gh pr view` and pass
  --branch to `gh run list` so only that branch's runs are returned
- Fallback (no active runs) also scopes to the branch when PR_NUMBER is set

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update scripts/workflow/monitor-gh-actions.sh

commit.message:
feat(tokio-compat): add enter(), with_tokio_context, and with_tokio_context_sync APIs

Add AsupersyncRuntime::enter() for running synchronous closures with the
runtime's Cx installed as current. Add with_tokio_context() for running
async futures with Cx re-installed on every poll and cancellation-aware
completion semantics (returns None when cancelled before completion).
Add with_tokio_context_sync() for preserving any active Cx binding
through synchronous call boundaries.

These APIs enable downstream code (e.g. frankenterm connectors) to
transparently bridge Tokio-shaped async code into the Asupersync
execution model while preserving cancellation contracts.

- Add 4 focused unit tests covering Cx installation, value return,
  cancellation-before-start, and sync Cx preservation
- Import CancellationMode, CancelAware, CancelResult from cancel module

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: asupersync
repository.description: Async runtime for Rust where correctness is structural: region-owned tasks, cancel-correct protocols, capability-gated effects, and deterministic replay testing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: mitigate LLM hallucination on batch reaction approvals

Two-pronged fix for Haiku 4.5 skipping tool calls when processing
multiple thumbs-up reactions simultaneously:

1. Serialize reaction processing via threading.Lock so the LLM only
   sees one approval request at a time (zulip_channel.py)
2. Add workspace/AGENTS.md bootstrap file with explicit tool-call
   discipline rules, seeded to persistent volume on first boot
   (Dockerfile.fly, start.sh)

Also strengthens the reaction content message to require tool results
and increases reaction timeout from 30s to 120s.

commit.message:
Fix duplicate train lines caused by repeated entries in source dataset

The japan-train-data source stores many lines 2-4 times under the same
Japanese name and identical station lists. This caused stations like
Shinjuku-sanchome to show the Fukutoshin Line and Toei Shinjuku Line
twice each in the platform roll list.

Fix: deduplicate by Japanese line name (ja) when building data-tokyo.json
and in filterTokyoLines() as a safety net.

Before: 212 lines, 4137 stations, 315 KB
After:  146 lines, 2434 stations, 189 KB

Bumps cache to v6 so existing users get the clean deduplicated data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

C-1: costiTot() NaN guard — Number(v)||0 in reduce (v18.94)

Riga 299: Object.values(u.costi).reduce((s,v) => s+(Number(v)||0), 0)
Riga 303: stessa protezione in budgetCostiTot

Se un valore in u.costi è undefined o stringa, il precedente s+v
propagava NaN silenziosamente su tutta la Plancia. Con Number()||0
il valore malformato è trattato come 0 e il calcolo prosegue.
Zero impatto su dati corretti. TC 2025/2026/2027: 0 errori.

Add ephemeral project lifecycle (Priority 6)

Projects are ephemeral workspaces that group jobs and follow
active → archived → [deleted] lifecycle. Archive releases
presentation artifacts (polish_cache, view_refinements,
presentation_cache) while retaining engine outputs for revive.

Modified: job creation accepts project_id, auto-archive background task

commit.message:
Include izakayas in nearby venue search

Expands the Overpass query from bars/pubs only to a union that also
catches anything tagged cuisine=izakaya (the standard OSM tag for
izakayas, which are typically amenity=restaurant not amenity=bar).

- Uses union query: (bar|pub nodes) + (cuisine~izakaya nodes)
- Deduplicates results by OSM id in case a node matches both
- Per-type icons: 🏮 izakaya  🍻 pub  🍺 bar
- Type label shown in small text next to each name
- Updated card title, button icon, and empty-state message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace DROP CASCADE with TRUNCATE in populate_siteinfo to preserve dependent views

DROP TABLE siteinfo CASCADE was destroying vw_units_inventory and vw_reviews
every time the siteinfo pipeline ran, with nothing to recreate them. Switched
to TRUNCATE within a single transaction so dependent views are preserved and
the table is never left empty mid-refresh.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add player news integration via RotoBaller RSS and MLB Transactions API

Hybrid architecture: ETL cron loads news into SQLite for historical
queries, plus a live CLI tool for real-time freshness when the agent
evaluates players. This gives the agent contextual awareness (injury
details, IL moves, callups, DFAs, trades) that was missing from
keeper/trade recommendations.

New files:
- etl/extractors/news.py — shared fetch/parse for both sources
- agent/news.py — live CLI (player, transactions, headlines commands)

Modified:
- db/schema.sql — player_news table + indexes
- etl/load_data.py — register news source
- etl/transforms/players.py — news identity resolution block
- agent/prompt.py — table docs, CLI docs, query examples, behavioral instruction

Add CoS persistent personal life memory system

CoS now automatically extracts and permanently stores biographical life facts
from conversations (family relationships, deaths, milestones, health conditions).
Previously, personal context was only captured on conversation clear. Now it
runs after every message via background thread with regex pre-screening to
avoid unnecessary API calls.

New PersonalFact model provides structured, never-pruned storage. Facts are
injected into CoS system prompt alongside existing personal context. AI
extraction explicitly prevents guessing/hallucination — only extracts facts
the user clearly stated.
